pattern: Mirror the left half to form a candidate palindrome, then also try the prefix plus and minus one plus the all-nines and ten-power-one boundaries; the closest non-equal palindrome is among these few candidates.
hints: A palindrome is fixed by its first half, so the natural candidate mirrors the high half of the number onto the low half.
Mirroring may land above or below the input, so also consider raising and lowering the half by one before mirroring.

Handle the length-change edge cases too: the all-nines number one shorter and the one-followed-by-zeros-and-one number one longer; among all candidates pick the closest, breaking ties toward the smaller.

edge_inputs.description: single digit ties to the smaller side
all nines rolls to a longer palindrome
power of ten drops to all nines
ten ties between nine and eleven
large palindrome must move to a neighbor

stress.description: random integers of random length up to 7 digits
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    length = rng.randint(1, 7)
    first = rng.randint(1, 9)
    rest = ''.join(str(rng.randint(0, 9)) for _ in range(length - 1))
    return [str(first) + rest]
stress.note: no leading zeros, kept small enough for the brute oracle scan
